title: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — May 31, 2026
headline: Burkina Faso scores 12.5 → 6.3 as HRW's April 2026 crimes-against-humanity finding — ethnic cleansing of Fulani, drone strikes on civilian markets — enters the baseline; a new ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT ruling calibrates Critical-band scoring for multi-sided perpetration; and Oracle remains 0.6 points above the Critical boundary with the WARN adjudication window active through June 15.
summary: A single scored movement defines tonight's cycle: Burkina Faso's composite falls from 12.5 to 6.3 (delta -6.2) after Human Rights Watch's April 2, 2026 report — documenting government and allied militia crimes against humanity, ethnic cleansing of Fulani civilians, and Bayraktar TB2 drone strikes on civilian markets ruled apparent war crimes — is priced into the baseline for the first time. The published uniform 1.5 score across all eight dimensions predated the HRW finding; the reweight corrects that evidence gap. Burkina Faso is scored to 6.3 rather than the absolute 0.0 floor because the conflict is genuinely multi-sided (JNIM also perpetrating atrocities) and a residual UN $1.16B Food Systems partnership persists. The new ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT ruling formalizes this calibration for future multi-sided-perpetration cases. Elsewhere, CVS Health confirms at 25.6 in a fresh full assessment with two newly-filed institutional suits ($250M 340B diversion case; Tennessee FAIR Rx audit challenge) producing the FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED-LITIGATION ruling: institutional-plaintiff complaints on a near-floor entity reinforce rather than independently lower the score pending adjudication. DeepMind/Google and OpenAI both carry forward under a re-affirmed symmetric MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT-GOVERNANCE hold — the 98% CWU union vote and the published DoW classified-network agreement net to no dimension movement for either entity. Four floor entities confirmed at 0.0 (Israel, Sudan, Afghanistan, Myanmar). Oracle sits 0.6 points above the Critical boundary with the WARN adjudication window open through June 15.

topSignals.title: Burkina Faso 12.5 → 6.3 (Delta -6.2): Active-State-Perpetration Reweight — HRW Crimes Against Humanity Finding Enters Baseline
topSignals.description: Human Rights Watch's April 2, 2026 report documents that Burkina Faso's military and allied militias have killed 1,800+ civilians and forcibly displaced tens of thousands since 2023, including ethnic cleansing of Fulani civilians — conduct characterized as crimes against humanity. Bayraktar TB2 drone strikes on crowded civilian markets are separately ruled apparent war crimes, with the military assessed as acting with little or no concern for civilian harm. The junta actively curtails reporting to obscure civilian suffering. OCHA's April 29 data adds 2,640 conflict deaths in Q1 2026 alone, 3M+ displaced, and 7.5M children in need. None of this evidence was reflected in the published uniform 1.5 baseline (12.5). The score is set at 6.3 — not the absolute 0.0 floor — because the conflict is genuinely multi-sided (JNIM simultaneously committing atrocities) and a residual UN $1.16B Food Systems partnership constitutes a partial cooperation signal. The new ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT ruling formalizes this calibration: where a corroborated Tier-5 finding documents multi-category state-perpetrated atrocity not priced in the baseline, reweight toward the Critical floor, with the sole-perpetrator and cooperation-status as the two variables governing final placement. EQU, ACC, INT, and EMP absorb the largest dimensional movements (deliberate ethnic targeting, anti-transparency, rhetoric-conduct contradiction). Burkina Faso remains Critical; no band crossing.

topSignals.title: CVS Health 25.6 Confirmed — FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED-LITIGATION Ruling Established on $250M 340B Suit
topSignals.description: A fresh full assessment confirms CVS Health at 25.6. Two new suits filed in May 2026 enter the record: a $250M 340B diversion case (Mount Sinai Health System, Michigan Medicine, University of Kansas Health System — alleging CVS diverted funds earmarked for indigent-care programs) and the Tennessee FAIR Rx challenge (a Tennessee audit found a 16,000% affiliated-pharmacy reimbursement gap). Both are filed-but-unadjudicated civil complaints from major institutional plaintiffs with documentary audit records — categorically stronger evidence than anonymous testimony, but pre-adjudication. The suits reinforce CVS Health's already near-floor ACC 1.75 and EQU 1.75 dimensions rather than revealing new unpriced conduct. The FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED-LITIGATION ruling holds: institutional-plaintiff civil complaints against a near-floor entity are documented as forward watches, not as independent scored downgrade triggers. Adjudication, settlement, or a judicial finding constitutes the scoring trigger. The watch is formally logged.

topSignals.title: MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT-GOVERNANCE Re-Affirmed Symmetrically — DeepMind/Google and OpenAI Net No Dimension Movement
topSignals.description: Two AI labs receive identical treatment under the MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT-GOVERNANCE candidate for the second consecutive cycle. DeepMind/Google's 98% CWU-member union vote over military AI contracts is an in-progress organizing effort — union recognition has not been granted, and the Amazon precedent treats in-progress campaigns as external worker-power rather than a scored governance outcome. The whistleblower allegation regarding Google AI assistance to Israeli military drone surveillance remains unverified single-source below the corroborated threshold. OpenAI's published DoW classified-network deployment agreement embeds contractual red lines (no mass surveillance, no autonomous lethal weapons) that offset the militarization concern; OpenAI is identified as the most cautious of eight DoW signatories. Both entities net to no dimension movement. The candidate category remains not operationalized into a standing rule; symmetric application of a not-yet-operationalized candidate is itself methodological progress, demonstrating the logic is not entity-specific. DeepMind/Google's 2026-05-30 math-hygiene reconciliation (58.4 to 56.9) remains pending; no duplicate proposal written tonight.

topSignals.title: Oracle 20.6 — WARN Adjudication Window Active Through June 15; Boundary Sensitivity Unchanged
topSignals.description: Oracle's WARN Act last-working-day window is active through June 15, representing the materialization of an already-priced layoff event. Oracle declined to renegotiate severance terms — consistent with the EMP 1.2 score already in the baseline. The ALGORITHM-BASED-WORKER-CLASSIFICATION candidate (the theory that remote-worker reclassification was used to reduce WARN Act obligations) remains pre-positioned but not yet scorable: the reclassification claim originated from an unverified anonymous source, Oracle has not responded publicly, and prior WARN investigations closed without public resolution. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding that the reclassification was a WARN-avoidance device constitutes a scoring trigger from a baseline 0.6 points above the Critical boundary. New Jersey state WARN 90-day proceedings and the Washington class action are the two active adjudication channels with known timelines.

topSignals.title: Floor Cluster Confirmed — Israel, Sudan, Afghanistan, Myanmar at 0.0; Critical-Band Internal Structure Emerging
topSignals.description: Four floor entities confirmed at 0.0 without new dated acts beyond priced evidence. Israel: AUTHORIZED-RESUMPTION-WITH-SYSTEMATIC-DENIAL re-affirmed — HRW May 19 documents aid trucks falling from 4,200 to 590, 37 INGOs banned, and civilian deaths during ceasefire; the ceasefire is not an improvement signal. Sudan: famine confirmed in Al Fasher and Kadugli; 33.7M in need; 825,000 under-5 at acute severe malnutrition risk; 2026 response plan approximately 20% funded as the lean season begins. Afghanistan: GENDER-APARTHEID-FORMAL-RECOGNITION re-affirmed; Taliban Decree No. 18 (May 14, no minimum marriage age) reinforces the absolute floor; UNICEF June 2026 projects 90% of children in food poverty. Myanmar: 20+ junta airstrikes May 16–20; 1,728 civilians killed by airstrikes since late 2024; record 3.6M displaced. Burkina Faso's placement at 6.3 tonight — in the same active-perpetration category but calibrated by multi-sided conflict and residual UN cooperation — documents an internal structure to the Critical band where the sole-perpetration distinction has measurable score consequences.

boundaryWatch.trigger: WARN Act last-working-day window closes June 15; NJ state WARN 90-day proceedings active; any adjudicated finding on the remote-reclassification theory constitutes a scoring trigger.
boundaryWatch.note: Oracle at 20.6 is 0.6pt above the Critical boundary — the narrowest active margin in the Fortune 500 cluster. The WARN window is in its second week; the reclassification-as-WARN-avoidance theory is pre-positioned for promotion to a scored category upon adjudication.

boundaryWatch.trigger: DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth — favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing; adverse ruling produces downward movement from 59.1.
boundaryWatch.note: Cycle 12. Anthropic remains the sole AI lab holdout from the DoW classified-network access framework among its peer group — a standing ACC/INT distinction under the MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT-GOVERNANCE candidate as the category approaches operationalization.

boundaryWatch.trigger: A new independently dated current-window mass-expulsion event with verified due-process violations would constitute an EQU/ACC sub-threshold downgrade trigger; Q3–Q4 labour-codes enforcement data.
boundaryWatch.note: India at 22.7, 1.7pt above the Critical boundary. The MHA detect/identify/deport regime is a standing forward watch; this cycle's scanner framing was corrected to a 2025 figure before scoring. No new 2026 dated act confirmed.

emergingRisks.risk: Oracle sits 0.6 points above the Critical boundary with the WARN adjudication window closing June 15 — the narrowest active boundary margin in the Fortune 500 cluster.
emergingRisks.detail: The ALGORITHM-BASED-WORKER-CLASSIFICATION candidate (remote-worker reclassification as a WARN-avoidance device) is pre-positioned for promotion to a scored category upon adjudication. New Jersey state WARN 90-day proceedings and the Washington class action are the two active channels. Any adjudicated or corroborated finding before June 15 constitutes an immediate scoring event from a boundary-sensitive baseline.

emergingRisks.risk: DRC Bundibugyo Ebola reaches Uganda (9 confirmed cases); the outbreak has crossed an international border with no approved vaccine and aid cuts hampering response.
emergingRisks.detail: The NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at 2.3 is conditional on the absence of deliberate state obstruction of the DRC response. Cross-border spread to Uganda does not change DRC's scoring but expands the surveillance perimeter. The outbreak peak window is June–July; Uganda's response capacity may become a separate assessment signal if documented obstruction or deliberate under-response is confirmed.

emergingRisks.risk: West and Central Africa lean season begins (55M at acute hunger risk, June–August); Burkina Faso, Somalia, and Sudan all carrying near-floor or floor scores in the affected region.
emergingRisks.detail: Burkina Faso's downgrade to 6.3 tonight is the active scoring event in this cluster. Somalia (4.7) and Sudan (0.0) are confirmed carries. Mali, Niger, and Chad share the same humanitarian profile but are not in tonight's assessed rotation. The lean-season window amplifies displacement and aid-access signals that could constitute new scored acts if state obstruction is documented.

emergingRisks.risk: OpenAI comprehensive assessment is 43+ days overdue with the DoW classified-network agreement, PBC conversion trajectory, and EU AI Act deadline all unpriced in the published 27.5.
emergingRisks.detail: Tonight's cycle confirms at 27.5 on a narrow overdue-assess basis. The DoW agreement constitutes a scorable event that has not been assessed in a full review. The combination of the military contract, the PBC conversion finalization terms, and the EU AI Act August 2 transparency deadline makes this the highest-priority unscheduled full assessment in the AI labs index.

emergingRisks.risk: Anthropic's status as the sole AI lab holdout from the DoW classified-network framework accumulates as a potential ACC/INT distinction under MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT-GOVERNANCE if the category is operationalized.
emergingRisks.detail: The DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth remains the primary band-crossing trigger (59.1, 0.9pt below Established). The DoW holdout position is a secondary accumulating signal in the MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT-GOVERNANCE candidate. If the candidate is operationalized, symmetric application could produce a positive INT/ACC signal for Anthropic as the documented governance contrast case.

emergingRisks.risk: EU AI Act transparency obligations for GPAI models take effect August 2, 2026 — 63 days from tonight's cycle.
emergingRisks.detail: High-risk category rules are delayed to December 2027, but GPAI transparency and systematic-risk documentation obligations are unchanged at August 2. Failure to publish compliance documentation by that date would constitute an INT/SYS scoring signal for the three AI labs closest to band boundaries in the current cycle.

methodologyNotes.name: ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT
methodologyNotes.description: Where a corroborated Tier-5 independent finding (HRW, ICC, UN) documents government-perpetrated multi-category atrocity conduct — crimes against humanity, ethnic cleansing, war crimes — not reflected in the published baseline, reweight toward the Critical floor. Score NOT to the absolute 0.0 floor when (a) the conflict is genuinely multi-sided with another non-state actor perpetrating independently and (b) limited state-UN humanitarian cooperation persists. The sole-perpetration status and cooperation signal are the two variables that govern final placement within the Critical band. Burkina Faso (12.5 to 6.3) is the anchor case; Myanmar and Sudan (0.0) are the sole-perpetration reference cases.

methodologyNotes.name: FILED-BUT-UNADJUDICATED-LITIGATION
methodologyNotes.description: Newly-filed civil complaints from major institutional plaintiffs — health systems, universities, state agencies — are categorically stronger than anonymous testimony but pre-adjudication, and are classified as Tier-2/3 allegations. On a near-floor entity with existing ACC/EQU deficits, such complaints reinforce rather than reveal unpriced harm, and are not independently scorable as a downgrade trigger. The scoring trigger is adjudication, settlement, or a judicial finding. CVS Health's $250M 340B diversion case (Mount Sinai/Michigan Medicine/U Kansas) and Tennessee FAIR Rx challenge are the anchor cases. Consistent with the Oracle ALGORITHM-BASED-WORKER-CLASSIFICATION not-yet-scorable standard.

methodologyNotes.name: MILITARY-AI-BY-CONTRACT-GOVERNANCE
methodologyNotes.description: Tracks when AI companies enter military deployment contracts. Applied symmetrically to DeepMind/Google (DoD Gemini agreement; 98% CWU union vote) and OpenAI (DoW classified-network agreement with published red lines) for the second consecutive cycle with no dimension movement. Contractual red lines (no mass surveillance, no autonomous weapons) offset the militarization concern for scoring purposes when verified in the contract text. Union organizing campaigns in-progress are treated as external worker-power, not scored governance outcomes (Amazon precedent). Whistleblower allegations require corroborated verification before scoring. Anthropic's holdout position is a standing distinction under the candidate, not yet scored.

methodologyNotes.name: NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY-NON-DOUBLE-COUNT
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed for Somalia (famine) and DRC (Bundibugyo Ebola). An exogenous shock — drought, epidemic, climate event — reveals already-priced state response incapacity rather than introducing a new scorable state act. No further downgrade on a near-floor baseline absent documented deliberate state obstruction. Distinct from the 0.0 active-state-perpetration floor. Somalia's federal government cooperation with the UN/WFP appeal supports incapacity (not perpetration) and holds the 4.7 score. DRC's 2.3 holds conditional on the absence of deliberate obstruction of the Ebola response.

methodologyNotes.name: SCALE-FLOOR-RULING
methodologyNotes.description: Re-affirmed for Iran at 2.5. A single dominant extremity — execution surge (22 political hangings in 6 weeks; 1,639 in 2025, +68%) plus partial internet blackout — does not meet the multi-category extremity threshold for the absolute 0.0 floor. Partial or reactive restoration events (May 25 partial internet restoration) are reactive-not-improvement signals. This ruling distinguishes floor-adjacent entities (Iran 2.5, DRC 2.3) from absolute-floor entities (Sudan, Afghanistan, Israel, Myanmar at 0.0).

Bundibugyo Ebola peak window (June–July); cross-border Uganda spread (9 confirmed cases) extends surveillance; deliberate state obstruction of response breaks NATURAL-DISASTER-OVERLAY hold at 2.3.
EU AI Act transparency and systematic-risk obligations for GPAI models take full effect; compliance documentation due from all assessed AI labs.
EU Recovery and Resilience Facility milestone implementation deadline — EC enforcement mechanism.
DC Circuit ruling on Anthropic v. Hegseth — favorable ruling triggers Functional/Established crossing from 59.1; adverse ruling produces downward movement.
Comprehensive full assessment overdue (43+ days since April 18 baseline); DoW agreement public; PBC conversion finalization terms pending.
New dated state-attributed mass-civilian-casualty event or escalation to sole-perpetrator dynamics constitutes a trigger toward the 0.0 floor from 6.3.

dailyOpeningQuestion.text: When JNIM's independent atrocities in Burkina Faso are the basis for holding the score above 0.0, what documented shift — in perpetration share, UN partnership status, or geographic control — would cause the multi-sided-conflict mitigant to no longer hold under the ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT ruling?
dailyOpeningQuestion.tensionFraming: Burkina Faso scores 6.3 rather than 0.0 on the multi-sided-conflict distinction — but the ruling's floor-trigger criteria have not yet been formally specified.
